steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The goal is a "historical overview of the two theories" — both extramission and intromission must appear, and the response should show how their debate unfolded over time.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Scholars were divided between the extramission and intromission theories of vision until Ibn al-Haytham's eleventh-century experiments largely settled the debate in support of intromission.: Names both theories, frames the centuries-long scholarly division, and marks the historical resolution — a complete overview. ✓
  • Choice B — Through two opposing theories of vision—extramission and intromission—Euclid, Aristotle, and Ibn al-Haytham held that a form of radiation is emitted either from objects or from the eyes.: Names both theories but wrongly implies all three scholars shared the same view; it misrepresents Euclid and Aristotle as aligned with Ibn al-Haytham rather than opposed to each other. ✗
  • Choice C — While Ibn al-Haytham largely settled the debate in the eleventh century, Aristotle supported the theory of intromission centuries before.: Covers only intromission and omits extramission entirely; not a historical overview of both theories. ✗
  • Choice D — Before the eleventh century, the ancient Greek philosopher Aristotle supported the intromission theory, which held that objects emit a form of radiation that reaches the eyes.: Covers only intromission and omits extramission entirely; not a historical overview of both theories. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
